DISMISSAL WITH PREJUDICE OF COMPLAINT AS TO DEFENDANT HERTZ VEHICLES LLC

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE IS DENIED. THE NOTICE OF MOTION FAILS TO STATE THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR THE MOTION. IN ANY EVENT, IT IS UNTIMELY IN THAT MS. MAPLES RELIES ON MS. NORTON'S RESPONSES FROM FEBRUARY 2012, AND SHE HAS NOT PROVIDED ANY REASON FOR HER DELAY IN SEEKING TRANSFER EARLIER. FURTHER, MS. MAPLES HAS NOT MADE A SUFFICIENT SHOWING THAT THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES OR THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WILL BE PROMOTED BY THE CHANGE IN VENUE. (CCP 397(C).) ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO KINGS COUNTY IS DENIED. AT THIS STAGE OF THE CASE, THE ONLY GROUND FOR CHANGE OF VENUE THAT APPEARS TO BE POTENTIALLY APPLICABLE VENUE IS PROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO COUNTY SINCE THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES THAT HERTZ OPERATES AND DOES BUSINESS IN SAN FRANCISCO. TRANSFER ON THE BASIS OF THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES IS PREMATURE BECAUSE MS. MAPLES HAS NOT FILED HER ANSWER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR # 7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' MOTION TO CHANGE VENUE TO KINGS COUNTY IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 31, 2013 TO GIVE THE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. THE OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF ITS PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302 WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE NO LATER THAN 4PM ON DECEMBER 28. THE COURT HAS THE MOVING PAPERS. calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-28-2013 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jun-11-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-12-2013 Off Calendar

Ntc Of Mtn To Change Venue
Off Calendar Jan-31-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Edward J. Rodzewich, Es. of Stratman Patterson & Hunter, Tel. 510.457.3460, for defendant and moving party;
Brendan Gilbert, Esq. of R. Rex Parris Law Firm, Tel. 661.949.2595, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' Motion To Change Venue is denied. The notice of motion fails to state the statutory basis for the motion. In any event, it is untimely in that Ms. Maples relies on Ms. Norton's responses from February 2012, and she has not provided any reason for her delay in seeking transfer earlier. Further, Ms. Maples has not made a sufficient showing that the convenience of witnesses or the ends of justice will be promoted by the change in venue. (CCP 397(c).) Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn To Change Venue
Off Calendar Dec-27-2012 Continued To Jan-31-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:
DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' Motion To Change Venue to Kings County is continued to January 31, 2013 to give the opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. The opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of its papers to department 302 with a cover letter stating the new hearing date no later than 4pm on December 28. The Court has the moving papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/mjm

Ntc Of Mtn To Change Venue; Proof Of Service
Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT A. PARRIS, TEL. NO. (661) 949-2595 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CAROLIN MAPLES' MOTION To Change Venue to Kings County IS Denied. At this stage of the case, the only ground for change of venue that appears to be potentially applicable venue is proper in San Francisco County since the complaint alleges that Hertz operates and does business in San Francisco. Transfer on the basis of the convenience of witnesses is premature because Ms. Maples has not filed her answer. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK
